title: Commit Validation Job Tracking and Partial Commit Control
description: Validate candidate configuration, inspect PAN-OS job records, and commit full or partial configuration scope with explicit queue awareness.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook controls how Palo Alto candidate configuration becomes running configuration using #validate full, #show jobs all, #show jobs id, and #commit partial.

  1. Validate first: run the candidate configuration through PAN-OS validation before activation.
  2. Inspect queue state: review job IDs, warnings, and completion status from the job subsystem.
  3. Activate carefully: commit full scope or only the intended partial scope to reduce blast radius.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Validate candidate configuration
> configure
# validate full

# Step 2: Review job queue state
> show jobs all
> show jobs id <job-id>

# Step 3: Commit validated changes
> configure
# commit
# commit partial admin <admin> vsys <vsys-name> device-and-network excluded
# commit partial device-and-network excluded
prerequisites: CLI configuration privileges on the firewall or Panorama node
Existing candidate configuration changes to validate or commit
detectionNotes: Audit all validation and commit activity
Unexpected partial commits deserve change-control review

steps.name: Enter Candidate Config Context
steps.tooltip: PAN-OS separates candidate configuration from the running state to support controlled validation and commit.
steps.description: The operator enters configuration mode and targets the candidate configuration that is awaiting activation.

steps.name: Validate Candidate Configuration
steps.tooltip: Validation catches commit failures before they affect the running configuration.
steps.description: The candidate configuration is submitted to the PAN-OS validation engine for syntax and semantic verification.

steps.name: Queue Validation Job
steps.tooltip: The queue provides visibility into pending, running, completed, and failed validation work.
steps.description: PAN-OS assigns a job ID and tracks the validation operation through the management job queue.

steps.name: Review Validation Results
steps.tooltip: This step exposes dependency warnings and queue status before any running-config change occurs.
steps.description: The operator inspects job output, warnings, and completion state to confirm whether the candidate configuration is safe to activate.
steps.cli:
> show jobs all
> show jobs id <job-id>

steps.name: Activate Full or Partial Scope
steps.tooltip: Partial commit limits what is activated when multiple admins or configuration domains are involved.
steps.description: Validated candidate changes are committed into the running configuration, either as a full commit or a restricted partial commit.
steps.cli:
# commit
# commit partial admin <admin> vsys <vsys-name> device-and-network excluded
# commit partial device-and-network excluded

steps.name: Track Commit Execution
steps.tooltip: Commit operations are queued and processed alongside other long-running management tasks.
steps.description: The commit engine runs through the job subsystem until the configuration reaches a terminal success or failure state.

steps.name: Confirm Final Commit Outcome
steps.tooltip: The workflow ends only after the queue shows a definitive final result.
steps.description: The operator confirms whether the commit completed successfully and whether any follow-up correction is needed.
steps.cli:
> show jobs all
> show jobs id <job-id>
